methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting physical, chemical, and biological samples from the Iskar and Vit rivers for environmental and ichthyological studies.

steps.description: Sampling on the Vit and Iskar rivers was conducted on 1st and 5th October 2017, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field sampling on specified dates

steps.description: At each sampling site, physical and chemical parameters, such as water temperature, pH, oxygen concentration and conductivity, were measured using portable devices (Hanna Inc.).
steps.searchable_summary: measure physical and chemical parameters using portable devices

steps.description: Water velocity was measured with a portable water flow probe (model FP101, Global Water Instrumentation, Inc., USA).
steps.searchable_summary: measure water velocity using a portable flow probe

steps.description: Fish and macroinvertebrate samples were collected for an ichthyological study using electrofishing (SAMUS, 200/350 V, 3/12 A, 45-50 Hz) and a Hess sampler (ISO 8265:1988; frame size 0.3 by 0.3 m; mesh size 0.5 mm).
steps.searchable_summary: collect fish and macroinvertebrate samples using electrofishing and Hess sampler

methods.description: This method outlines the processing and identification of Chironomidae larvae and pupae from benthic samples and fish gut contents.

steps.description: From the obtained benthic samples and fish gut contents, Chironomidae larvae and pupae were sorted and identified.
steps.searchable_summary: sort and identify Chironomidae larvae and pupae from samples

steps.description: Head capsules of the larvae were mounted on permanent slides with Swann's solution.
steps.searchable_summary: mount larvae head capsules on slides with Swann's solution

steps.description: Individuals of Nubensia nubens were identified using the larva description by Spies and Dettinger-Klemm (2015).
steps.searchable_summary: identify Nubensia nubens using larva description

steps.description: The identified material was deposited in the Dipterological collection of the Department of General and Applied Hydrobiology, Sofia University, Bulgaria.
steps.searchable_summary: deposit identified material in university collection
